channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Let the games begin: after October the battle of the next-gen processors kicks off, so you might wanna hold off on building that new PC
item.description: Further rumors suggest that it might be true that an announcement for AMD’s Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU will follow Intel’s launch of its 200K Arrow Lake-S chip. Come October, gamers will have their pick of the best CPUs of this generation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Rare boss Craig Duncan to take over as Xbox Game Studios' head in November
item.description: Craig Duncan, head of Sea of Thieves studio Rare, will take over as the Xbox Game Studios boss later this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 8 easy ways to reuse commands on Linux
item.description: Rerunning commands on the Linux command line isn’t hard, but there are a number of ways to do it. This post provides eight examples of how to repeat commands on Linux, with and without changes. 1.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: El 67% del sector industrial ya utiliza la IA para la fabricación de productos
item.description: Desde su irrupción, la inteligencia artificial (IA) se ha perfilado como una herramienta poderosa para transformar el sector industrial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China unveils its plans to turn its feats in space exploration into scientific advances
item.description: China has made great strides in exploring space in recent years, rocketing astronauts to its own space station and bringing back rocks from the moon.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Convergenza IT-business: la digital transformation diventa trasformazione aziendale
item.description: Se le imprese oggi non possono che essere aziende digitali, è chiaro che il CIO è anche un manager che “sa di business”. Quando si parla di digitale, infatti, si parla di processi e, quindi, di ruoli e attività che definiscono il modello organizzativo e di ricavo dell’impresa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kenya moves 50 elephants to a larger park, says it's a sign poaching is low
item.description: Kenya is moving 50 elephants from a national reserve to a larger park after overpopulation overwhelmed the ecosystem, in what has been hailed as a conservation success.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Komu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Adobe makes GenStudio app generally available
item.description: Adobe’s GenStudio content supply chain platform is now generally available, with the ability to publish content directly to social media channels such as Instagram, Snap and TikTok coming soon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI being used to run world’s first indoor vertical berry farm
item.description: First indoor vertical berry farm opens in Virginia, using AI to grow 4 million pounds annually. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a closer look at the "growing" trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: A tech consultant has gone on trial over a year after the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top 5 methods to equip your car with Wi-Fi
item.description: Having Wi-Fi has become a necessity for many of us. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the top five ways to equip your vehicles with Wi-Fi.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Connect in a crisis with iOS 18's new video call feature for emergency services
item.description: Apple’s new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows users to share live video footage of the emergency directly with first responders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats press tech leaders to curb hurricane misinformation
item.description: A group of House Democrats representing areas affected by hurricanes Helene and Milton are calling on the leaders of major technology companies to do more to combat the flurry of misinformation online about the extreme weather. In a letter sent Friday, Reps. Deborah Ross (N.C.), Kathy Castor (Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple is inventing a reality distortion field
item.description: Apple didn’t cast much light on visionOS at WWDC this year, and it hasn’t received much attention since. But don’t mistake this something for nothing. Two recent events indicate there’s a lot going on behind the scenes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Lesser-known xargs command is a versatile time saver
item.description: The xargs command may be one that many Linux users don’t know, but it’s also one that can save a lot of time and trouble on the command line. It’s an unusual command with some very handy uses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI
item.description: The Army is exploring the idea of creating a separate path or a sub-path within the software acquisition pathway specifically for AI. The post DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs
item.description: Michael Berkholtz, a senior manager for technology lifecycle services for GSA, said the agency is analyzing emerging technologies based on four criteria. The post GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Someone reinvented the toilet so that you can do your business anywhere
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how the Generation 2 Reinvented Toilet transforms waste into safe materials, a promising sanitation solution that could transform lives and communities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WebSockets under the hood with Node.js
item.description: WebSockets is a network communication protocol that enables two-way client-server communication. WebSockets are often used for applications that require instantaneous updates, using a persistent duplex channel atop HTTP to support real-time interactions without constant connection negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with four features so far proposed for the release: a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface), and a late barrier expans
item.dc:creator: Info World
